Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
RYAN KRAFT, ESQ., VENARDI ZURADA LLP (925) 937-3900 rkraft@vzlawfirm.com Attorney for Plaintiffs JANE DOE AND MADISON FERNSTROM
MEGHAN K. LAWRENCE, ESQ., SIMS, LAWRENCE & BROGHAMMER (916) 797-8881 meghan@sims-law.net Attorney for Defendant ST. ANTHONY FOUNDATION (Erroneously sued herein as SAINT ANTHONYS FOUNDATION)

Hearing is held, NOT ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ST. ANTHONY FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant St. Anthony Foundation's demurrer to the ninth cause of action for negligent hiring and supervision and the tenth cause of action for respondeat superior alleged against it by plaintiff Jane Doe is overruled as to the ninth cause of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the tenth cause of action. As to both causes of action St. Anthony argues that its demurrer should be sustained because of misjoinder of the parties and failure to state a claim. St. Anthony's misjoinder argument lacks merit as to both causes of action since both are based on a single incident of alleged rape that Jane Doe alleges all defendants are liable for due to their negligent hiring and supervision. The ninth cause of action is adequately alleged based on the alleged ultimate facts that the alleged rapist had a history of violent criminal conduct and St. Anthony failed to implement proper prescreening and training, knew or should have known that the alleged rapist was violent and dangerous, and failed to supervise the alleged rapist. The tenth cause lacks sufficient ultimate facts to support respondeat superior liability. The allegation that the rape occurred while the alleged rapist was in the course and scope of his employment with St. Anthony is conclusory and unsupported by any ultimate facts. If she can do so in good faith, Jane Doe is granted leave to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support a viable claim for respondeat superior liability.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
